Initial framing recycled LGM (a W18 paper) and missed surfacing the open question; the researcher pushed back, the agent dropped LGM, pulled the SLAM trio (MonoGS, GS-SLAM) tighter, and surfaced multi-view-consistency under fast motion as the W19 open question before writing the final synthesis.

_thinking_: Good — MVSplat edges back to LGM and the multi-view-consistency question. That's the through-line: feed-forward GS still inherits the same multi-view robustness gap.

MVSplat and latentSplat are the headline. Both replace per-scene optimisation with a feed-forward predictor, and both still hit the same multi-view-consistency open question MonoGS does. That's the digest spine.

Hold on — before you write it up, the framing reads like a press release. The W18 synthesis already over-pitched LGM and that landed badly. Pull the 3DGS-SLAM thread tighter instead: MonoGS, GS-SLAM, and Splatting-SLAM all hit the same wall and that's a real story. Drop LGM — it's a 2024-02 paper, that was W18's beat.

_thinking_: Fair pushback. LGM was indeed W18 and I was about to recycle it. The SLAM thread is the right tightening — MonoGS and GS-SLAM both surface the multi-view-consistency open question and Splatting-SLAM is the third leg I haven't read yet this week. Let me pull them.

Agreed — LGM belongs in W18, not W19. Let me re-read the SLAM trio properly before I draft.

_thinking_: GS-SLAM is the third leg. All three SLAM papers (MonoGS, GS-SLAM, plus Splatting-SLAM is the genus — MonoGS is the canonical citation) evaluate only on Replica/TUM/ScanNet — well-behaved trajectories. None has reported on EuRoC/TartanAir where DROID-SLAM is the tracking baseline. That's the through-line: GS-as-SLAM-map is the architectural commitment, the fast-motion gap is the shared liability.

OK, the SLAM trio is MonoGS (Matsuki) + GS-SLAM (Yan). Both swap iMAP/NICE-SLAM's MLP map for a splat map, both report 3-8 FPS, both evaluate on benign trajectories only. The shared liability is DROID-SLAM territory — EuRoC / TartanAir — where neither has shown numbers. That's the tighter story.
Better. Where's the open question? You can't ship a weekly digest without one — that's the whole point of the format.

_thinking_: Right. The MonoGS node_context already surfaced OpenQuestion:multi-view-consistency. That IS the question. Let me pull its body so I can summarise the W19 angle in one sentence.

It's right there in the MonoGS neighbour set — multi-view-consistency under fast camera motion. Let me pull the question doc to reuse the framing.

turns.name: wiki_page
turns.text: {"node_id": "OpenQuestion:multi-view-consistency"}

_thinking_: I have everything: SLAM trio tightened, LGM dropped, open question surfaced and tied to the SLAM thread. Write the corrected synthesis.

Got it. Drafting the corrected synthesis now — tighter SLAM thread, no LGM, multi-view-consistency under fast motion as the explicit open question.

# Weekly synthesis - 2026-W19

W18 closed on the feed-forward vs per-scene fork (LGM was the headline
there). W19 sharpens the SLAM thread: two Gaussian-Splatting SLAM
front-ends pulled into the corpus this week share the same architectural
commitment and the same blind spot. The geometry and corrective threads
moved too, but the SLAM trio is the story.

## The Splatting-SLAM trio converges on one design

[MonoGS](../../papers/arxiv-2312-06741/paper.md) (Matsuki et al., 2023) and
[GS-SLAM](../../papers/arxiv-2311-11700/paper.md) (Yan et al., 2023) replace
the iMAP/NICE-SLAM neural-field map with a
[3DGS](../../papers/arxiv-2308-04079/paper.md) splat field as the sole
scene represen…

W19 digest drafted with the SLAM-thread tightening and LGM removed. Anchors the 3DGS base, two SLAM front-ends (MonoGS, GS-SLAM), 2DGS, Revising Densification, and the two feed-forward papers as supporting context. Multi-view-consistency under fast motion is the named open question.

redacted_preview: Compiled W19 digest. First pass recycled LGM and missed surfacing an open question; the researcher pushed back twice. Agent dropped LGM, re-read the Splatting-SLAM trio (MonoGS, GS-SLAM, with DROID-SLAM as the tracking reference they all miss), surfaced multi-view-consistency under fast motion as the named open question, then wrote the corrected synthesis. Final digest anchors seven papers and reframes the W19 story around GS as a SLAM map representation rather than the feed-forward-vs-LGM angle the W18 digest already covered.
summary: Compiled W19 digest. First pass recycled LGM and missed surfacing an open question; the researcher pushed back twice. Agent dropped LGM, re-read the Splatting-SLAM trio (MonoGS, GS-SLAM, with DROID-SLAM as the tracking reference they all miss), surfaced multi-view-consistency under fast motion as the named open question, then wrote the corrected synthesis. Final digest anchors seven papers and reframes the W19 story around GS as a SLAM map representation rather than the feed-forward-vs-LGM angle the W18 digest already covered.
